title: Dependency Graphs, Incremental Builds, Caches, and Watch Mode · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers dependency graphs, incremental builds, caches, and watch mode into a changed clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Record source, metadata, template, include, collection, taxonomy, route, asset, configuration, and tool-version dependencies per output.
Key cached stages by canonical input bytes, relevant configuration, algorithm and tool versions, schema, and platform boundary.
Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Dependency Graph
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 6413 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Dependency Graph.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record source, metadata, template, include, collection, taxonomy, route, asset, configuration, and tool-version dependencies per output. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Dependency Graph, keep Dependency Graph connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-clean-equivalence-iaa6cx
# Competency: ssg-clean-equivalence.
# Case 4551: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_clean_equivalence_iaa6cx(outputs: int = 20, dependency_nodes: int = 37, invalidated: int = 6, stale_outputs: int = 0, clean_digest_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_iaa6cx = "iaa6cx-xc6aai-4551"
    assert evidence_variant_iaa6cx
    evidence_axes_iaa6cx = (
        "readeriaa6cx",
        "source4551",
        "parsexc6aai",
        "routeiaa6cx4551",
        "artifact4551iaa6cx",
        "recoveryxc6aai4551",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_iaa6cx)) == 6
    if outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs:
        return False, dependency_nodes, "dependency-shape"
    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:
        return False, invalidated, "invalidation-scope"
    if stale_outputs > 0:
        return False, stale_outputs, "stale-output"
    if not clean_digest_equal:
        return False, invalidated, "clean-drift"
    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"
